schema_version: v0 — component-charter shape; migrates into the intention envelope when MAP.md Q6 lands
remit: THE EXPLICIT REMIT (Akien, verbatim): 'Its job is to save you tokens in exploring the issue in the same way the prebuild step does for coding. And to keep getting better over time.' The inspector is the DEBUGGING ANALOG OF THE PREBUILD STEP: prebuild pre-processes so CC (the expensive resolver) spends tokens only on the novel coding; the inspector pre-processes a red so CC spends tokens only on the novel diagnosis — never on a second exploration to gather data the first pass should have carried (Law 1, the cocoon). 'Gets better over time' is monotonic and physical: every gap it learns is one fewer second-exploration forever, so the tokens it saves only rise. Those two sentences ARE the falsifier — everything below serves them or it is dead weight.
what: THE SMART READER of DiagnosticBase's dumb breadcrumbs, factored (Akien) into the three parts of the diagnostic surface. (1) LOGGING — gates emit microsecond-stamped breadcrumbs (cairn/base/diagnostic.py); that is STATE, so its durable home is instance-space (~/.cairn/, cache-class json log). Mailbox here is the v0 in-memory STAND-IN for that log — a proof fixture so an emitter->receiver->inspector chain runs end to end, NEVER the runtime home. (2) CALLBACK — when a watched thing happens (a red, a watched gate-contact) a callback FIRES 'inspect now'; event, not poll (the shrinking-footprint discipline). (3) INSPECTOR — this module. It REACTS to the callback: applies FILTERS to the log and produces FINDINGS. FILTERS are predicates (predicate(record)->bool) conjoined at inspection time inside a fired run (never a standing scan/firehose): by_pointer (the primary — one item's whole transaction, the complete-first-pass slice) and by_gate are built because they have consumers; by_time_band / by_severity are filed edges the abstraction admits without a query-language built ahead of one. FINDINGS are json whose entire audience is CC: the ordered, isolated transaction slice (every emitted value carried, source deep-copied never mutated) PLUS the COMPLETENESS finding.
why: Instantiates the concept-piece I-complete-diagnostic-on-first-pass — findings that resolve the issue on the FIRST pass, a method Akien has proven over decades (2007 test framework, UU, Trouble Tickets; his first-reach debugging tool; half-day->10min). DiagnosticBase.emit left the intelligence OUT of the breadcrumb deliberately (Law 1 — the diagnostic surface's inference compiled ONCE, here, the inference_domain move); this is that one place. The learning-loop the inspector CARRIES makes the findings self-teaching (I-learns-its-gates pointed at the diagnostic surface): completeness rises monotonically and no learned gap recurs — which is exactly 'gets better over time' made physics.
invoke: In code: from cairn.diagnostic_inspector import Inspector, by_pointer, by_gate, CompletenessRegistry, Mailbox. Wire a Mailbox as a DiagnosticBase receiver (dev.set_diagnostic_receiver(box)); on a fired callback, findings = Inspector(registry).inspect(box.records(), by_pointer(ptr)[, by_gate(g)]) — filters conjoin. Fold a miss: inspector.record_miss(gate, key) ('learned' first time, 'recurred' after). Persist learned completeness: inspector.registry.save(path) / CompletenessRegistry.load(path). Prove: python3 cairn/diagnostic_inspector/proofs/test_inspector.py (exit 0 = green).
node_class: code-seam — berths in cairn/ (class-space) beside the breadcrumbs DiagnosticBase.emit sends. Born as children a (the crawler) + b (the learning-loop) of the ticket, then REFACTORED (2026-07-24) from diagnostic_interpreter/assemble(records, pointer) to the inspector/filters/findings shape Akien named — the same proven engine, re-seated on the true intention (an inspector reacting to a callback, filters composing, findings for CC). Child c (the `cairn diagnose <pointer>` CLI face, via cairn-command) is the LEAST-USED secondary access path (Akien: 'of all the secondary access paths, almost certainly the one I'm least likely to use') — buildable, lowest priority; the engine is the substance.
how_it_learns: YES — learning is its WHOLE SECOND HALF and the 'gets better over time' half of the remit, not an add-on. The CompletenessRegistry the Inspector CARRIES across inspections IS a learns-its-gates instance whose endpoint is the diagnostic findings: its GATE is a transition-class, its EVIDENCE is a resolver hitting a gap the findings should have carried, and its CONFIDENCE-MOVE is folding that key into what the class's findings must include (record_miss). The special-class falsifier is the learning rule made physics: a FIRST miss is a feed-forward signal ('learned'); an already-learned key absent from a later findings is a RECURRENCE, surfaced LOUD (inspect's completeness.recurrences, complete=False) — the terminal falsifier, a Law 1 defect it refuses to let pass silently (the surface failed to save the tokens it had already learned to save). Distinct endpoint from the decision-autonomy store in CairnCommons/learning/ (which learns whether CC may ACT); kin, cross-referenced, NOT merged — a distinct store with a distinct key. Its horizon: where learned completeness durably lives (a commons git-JSON store vs CC's shim when shims land) — injected path today, real home a filed edge.
traces_to: THE REMIT (save CC tokens exploring an issue as prebuild does for coding; get better over time) — the charter's own first field, Akien-stated
I-complete-diagnostic-on-first-pass (the concept-piece this instantiates — the findings deliver all resolve-data on the first pass)
Law 1 (a second exploration to gather more failure data is re-deriving the settled — the waste the complete findings kills; and the inspector's inference is compiled ONCE here, the cocoon)

Law 7 SHARPENED (the diagnostic surface is not merely loud but COMPLETE on first emission; a learned recurrence is surfaced LOUD, never silent; the findings are a transient surface, the RESOLUTION CC produces is the record of truth)
I-learns-its-gates (the learning-loop is gate-learning pointed at the diagnostic surface — a distinct endpoint: what the findings must CARRY)
Law 6 (the emission stays a thin pointer; the inspector reads and never emits, never copies the ticket, never mutates its source records — the dumb-breadcrumb / smart-reader split)
cairn/base/diagnostic.py (DiagnosticBase.emit — the breadcrumb this crawls; its docstring named this inspector as 'the next build')
owner: CC owns the diagnostic_inspector code + its proof and gates writes to them. The inspector READS breadcrumbs and logs; it never emits and never owns the tickets it points at (Law 6 — owned data stays with its owner; only the pointer crosses, and the inspector follows it, it does not copy).
proof: proofs/test_inspector.py — eight teeth a hollow inspector could not pass: findings filter ONE pointer's transaction ordered by the 6th-decimal stamp and isolated (real DiagnosticBase emissions, other pointers excluded — Law 6); FILTERS COMPOSE (by_pointer AND by_gate narrows to the conjunction — 'filters' is plural, not a single hardcoded match); it reads without mutating (deep-copied slice); a first miss is 'learned' and folded forward so the next findings require it, routed THROUGH the Inspector (the inspector is what gets better — Law 1); a learned gap that recurs is complete=False and named in recurrences (LOUD, Law 7); a complete findings is clean; record_miss distinguishes 'learned' from 'recurred'; learned completeness save->load round-trips (a missing file is an empty registry, not an error). Green bare (python3) and sealed under the tester; VALIDATION persisted beside-code at validations/test_inspector.json.
falsifier: RED, by physics, on any of: (1) the findings are INCOMPLETE by construction — they drop an emitted value or bleed in another pointer's records (not a faithful, isolated slice); (2) filters do NOT compose — a second filter is ignored so the conjunction is a lie; (3) the learning-loop records a miss but never changes the next findings (learning theater — the fold is a no-op); (4) an already-learned gap recurs and the findings stay SILENT about it (the terminal falsifier — must be loud, Law 7); (5) the inspector grows an emit path (it becomes a second emitter, collapsing the dumb-breadcrumb / smart-reader split — Law 6); (6) it mutates its source records (it must read, never write, what it is handed); (7) the learned-completeness store is merged into the decision-autonomy gate-store (two distinct learnings collapsed); (8) the inspector runs as a STANDING SCAN rather than reacting to a fired callback (the firehose/poll the shrinking-footprint discipline rejects).
state_and_history: compiled to state.json (projected window) + history.json (append-only voyage) beside this charter — the charter-state-history split; this file stays pure intention+why, the operational voyage lives in the sidecars (Law 1/4/7)
filed_edges: (a) v0 reads the in-memory Mailbox (a proof fixture); the durable breadcrumb log (instance-space ~/.cairn/, cache-class) is the real logging home, read only when a real crawl demands it — this is WHY child c (cairn diagnose) is genuinely blocked, not merely low-priority: no durable source exists yet
(b) the CALLBACK that fires 'inspect now' (event, not poll) is named in the design but not yet wired to a real red — grown against the first real fired inspection
(c) the durable home for learned completeness (a commons git-JSON store vs CC's shim when shims land) is a filed edge — the store path is injected, proven against a temp
(d) by_time_band / by_severity filters — the abstraction admits them; build each when a crawl demands it
(e) the `cairn diagnose <pointer>` CLI face (child c) — the least-used secondary access path; buildable via cairn-command, lowest priority
(f) the concept-piece it instantiates is CAST but awaiting Akien's signature gate
gates: proof gate — proofs/test_inspector.py, runnable bare (python3) and under the tester. Durability gate: not done until proven AND the VALIDATION is persisted beside-code (validations/test_inspector.json) AND committed (git).
provenance: Born 2026-07-23 as children a+b of the diagnostic-interpreter ticket (instantiating the concept-piece I-complete-diagnostic-on-first-pass); the shallow-copy defect surfaced on the first proof run (the read-never-mutate tooth) and was fixed to a deep copy before the first seal (Law 3). REFACTORED 2026-07-24: Akien reframed the diagnostic as 'part logging, part callback, part inspector that reacts to that callback', with filters and findings, the CLI as a rarely-used secondary path, and the report as 'json intended for you [CC]' — then stated the explicit remit (save CC tokens as prebuild does; get better over time). The engine was proven; the refactor re-seated it on the true shape (diagnostic_interpreter->diagnostic_inspector, assemble->Inspector.inspect(log, *filters), report->findings) and added the compose-filters tooth. Records of truth from the interpreter life (history's early entries, the interpreter VALIDATION) STAY by Law 7 — the name-change is a new event appended, not an erasure.
